(ICE) is a [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music") ensemble, based in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). ICE performs a diverse and extensive array of chamber, electro-acoustic, improvisatory, and multimedia works.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
* *Ice* Dai Fujikura, International Contemporary Ensemble, Jayce Ogren, Matthew Ward (Kairos, 2013)[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* *Dinosaur Scar* Du Yun, International Contemporary Ensemble (Tudra, 2018)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

* *[On the Torment of Saints, the Casting of Spells and the Evocation of Spirits](/wiki/On_the_Torment_of_Saints,_the_Casting_of_Spells_and_the_Evocation_of_Spirits "On the Torment of Saints, the Casting of Spells and the Evocation of Spirits")* (Tzadik, 2013)
* *Son Of Chamber Symphony / String Quartet* St. Lawrence String Quartet ([Nonesuch](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records"), 2011)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Musical artist

The **International Contemporary Ensemble** (ICE) is a [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music") ensemble, based in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). ICE performs a diverse and extensive array of chamber, electro-acoustic, improvisatory, and multimedia works.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Contemporary_Ensemble&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The International Contemporary Ensemble was founded in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") in 2001 by [Claire Chase](/wiki/Claire_Chase "Claire Chase") (Ensemble flautist and former executive director).[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) The early ensemble—consisting primarily of alumni from the Oberlin Conservatory in Ohio—presented its first Chicago concert at the Three Arts Club in January 2002.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the following year, the Ensemble made its New York City debut at the [Miller Theatre](/wiki/Miller_Theatre "Miller Theatre").

Since its founding, the Ensemble has premiered over 500 compositions, many of these commissions and collaborations spawning from their noteworthy residency programs: the 21st Century Young Composers Project and ICElab.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In addition to commissioning emerging composers, the ensemble has also premiered numerous works by world-renowned composers, including [Georges Aperghis](/wiki/Georges_Aperghis "Georges Aperghis"), [Alvin Lucier](/wiki/Alvin_Lucier "Alvin Lucier"), [Pauline Oliveros](/wiki/Pauline_Oliveros "Pauline Oliveros"), [John Zorn](/wiki/John_Zorn "John Zorn"), [David Lang](/wiki/David_Lang_(composer) "David Lang (composer)"), [Liza Lim](/wiki/Liza_Lim "Liza Lim"), [Dai Fujikura](/wiki/Dai_Fujikura "Dai Fujikura"), [Chaya Czernowin](/wiki/Chaya_Czernowin "Chaya Czernowin"), [Julio Estrada](/wiki/Julio_Estrada_(musicologist) "Julio Estrada (musicologist)"), [Amir Shpilman](/wiki/Amir_Shpilman "Amir Shpilman"), [George E. Lewis](/wiki/George_E._Lewis "George E. Lewis"), [Anna Thorvaldsdottir](/wiki/Anna_Thorvaldsdottir "Anna Thorvaldsdottir"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) and Carla Kihlstedt, among others.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/International_Contemporary_Ensemble%2C_Mostly_Mozart_Festival%2C_August_22%2C_2013.jpg/250px-International_Contemporary_Ensemble%2C_Mostly_Mozart_Festival%2C_August_22%2C_2013.jpg)](/wiki/File:International_Contemporary_Ensemble,_Mostly_Mozart_Festival,_August_22,_2013.jpg)

ICE performing at the Mostly Mozart Festival on August 22, 2013.

The International Contemporary Ensemble has performed at the Lincoln Center Festival (New York), Musica Nova Helsinki (Finland), Wien Modern (Austria), Acht Brücken Music for Cologne (Germany), La Cité de la Musique (Paris), the [Darmstädter Ferienkurse](/wiki/Darmst%C3%A4dter_Ferienkurse "Darmstädter Ferienkurse") (Germany), Mostly Mozart Festival (New York), as well as tours of Japan, Brazil and France. They have held residencies at the [Museum of Contemporary Art Chicago](/wiki/Museum_of_Contemporary_Art,_Chicago "Museum of Contemporary Art, Chicago"), Mostly Mozart Festival, New York University, Columbia College (Chicago), and Conservatorio de las Rosas (Morelia, Mexico), among others.

The International Contemporary Ensemble is currently based in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), New York with [George E. Lewis](/wiki/George_E._Lewis "George E. Lewis") serving as their artistic director.

## Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Contemporary_Ensemble&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Members")]

ICE is a flexible collective of thirty-eight performing artists, including strings, woodwinds, piano, percussion, voice, composers, and light and sound designers.[[7]](#cite_note-7) As an artist-run organization, most of ICE's executive leadership, concert production, marketing, fundraising, technology and database systems are managed by members of the ensemble.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
